title: What is React.StrictMode?
description: What does wrapping your app in StrictMode do?
options: Activates additional development-only checks: warns about deprecated APIs, detects unexpected side effects, and double-invokes certain functions
correct_answer: Activates additional development-only checks: warns about deprecated APIs, detects unexpected side effects, and double-invokes certain functions

title: What is the difference between controlled and uncontrolled components?
description: How do controlled and uncontrolled form components differ?
options: Controlled: React state drives the input value via value + onChange. Uncontrolled: DOM manages state internally, accessed via ref
correct_answer: Controlled: React state drives the input value via value + onChange. Uncontrolled: DOM manages state internally, accessed via ref

title: What is the purpose of key prop in lists?
description: Why does React need key props on list items?
options: Keys help React identify which items changed, were added, or removed; they must be stable, unique siblings to ensure efficient reconciliation
correct_answer: Keys help React identify which items changed, were added, or removed; they must be stable, unique siblings to ensure efficient reconciliation

title: What is React.Fragment?
description: What does Fragment do?
options: Lets you group multiple children without adding an extra DOM node; shorthand is <>...</>
correct_answer: Lets you group multiple children without adding an extra DOM node; shorthand is <>...</>

title: What is the useReducer hook?
description: When should you use useReducer instead of useState?
options: useReducer is better for complex state logic with multiple sub-values or when next state depends on previous state; uses a reducer function like Redux
correct_answer: useReducer is better for complex state logic with multiple sub-values or when next state depends on previous state; uses a reducer function like Redux

title: What is the useRef hook?
description: What are common use cases for useRef?
options: Creates a mutable ref object that persists across renders without causing re-renders; used for DOM access, storing previous values, and instance variables
correct_answer: Creates a mutable ref object that persists across renders without causing re-renders; used for DOM access, storing previous values, and instance variables

title: What is prop drilling?
description: What is prop drilling and how is it solved?
options: Passing props through many intermediate components that don't use them; solved with Context API, state management libraries, or component composition
correct_answer: Passing props through many intermediate components that don't use them; solved with Context API, state management libraries, or component composition

title: What is the Context API?
description: How does React Context work?
options: createContext + Provider + useContext: provides a way to share values across the component tree without prop drilling
correct_answer: createContext + Provider + useContext: provides a way to share values across the component tree without prop drilling

title: What is code splitting in React?
description: How do you implement code splitting?
options: React.lazy() + Suspense for component-level splitting; dynamic import() for on-demand module loading — reduces initial bundle size
correct_answer: React.lazy() + Suspense for component-level splitting; dynamic import() for on-demand module loading — reduces initial bundle size

title: What is the virtual DOM diffing algorithm?
description: How does React's diffing (reconciliation) work?
options: React compares two virtual DOM trees using heuristic O(n) algorithm: different element types = full re-render; same type = update attributes; keys optimize list diffing
correct_answer: React compares two virtual DOM trees using heuristic O(n) algorithm: different element types = full re-render; same type = update attributes; keys optimize list diffing

title: What is the children prop?
description: What is props.children?
options: A special prop that contains child elements passed between opening and closing tags of a component; used for composition and wrapper patterns
correct_answer: A special prop that contains child elements passed between opening and closing tags of a component; used for composition and wrapper patterns

title: What is the useCallback hook?
description: When should you use useCallback?
options: Memoizes a function reference to prevent unnecessary re-creation; useful when passing callbacks to memoized child components
correct_answer: Memoizes a function reference to prevent unnecessary re-creation; useful when passing callbacks to memoized child components

title: What is the useMemo hook?
description: What does useMemo do?
options: Memoizes a computed value; only recalculates when dependencies change. Used for expensive computations to avoid recalculation on every render
correct_answer: Memoizes a computed value; only recalculates when dependencies change. Used for expensive computations to avoid recalculation on every render

title: What is the difference between useEffect and useLayoutEffect?
description: When should you use useLayoutEffect?
options: useLayoutEffect fires synchronously after DOM mutation but before browser paint; useEffect fires asynchronously after paint. useLayoutEffect for DOM measurements/mutations that must be visible immediately
correct_answer: useLayoutEffect fires synchronously after DOM mutation but before browser paint; useEffect fires asynchronously after paint. useLayoutEffect for DOM measurements/mutations that must be visible immediately

title: What is batching in React?
description: What is automatic batching in React 18?
options: React 18 groups multiple state updates into a single re-render for better performance — works everywhere including setTimeout, Promises, and native events (not just React events)
correct_answer: React 18 groups multiple state updates into a single re-render for better performance — works everywhere including setTimeout, Promises, and native events (not just React events)

title: What is server-side rendering (SSR)?
description: What is SSR in React?
options: Rendering React components to HTML on the server, sending fully-rendered HTML to the client for better SEO and faster first contentful paint
correct_answer: Rendering React components to HTML on the server, sending fully-rendered HTML to the client for better SEO and faster first contentful paint

title: What is hydration?
description: What does hydration mean in React?
options: The process of attaching event listeners and making server-rendered static HTML interactive on the client; React matches existing DOM instead of re-creating it
correct_answer: The process of attaching event listeners and making server-rendered static HTML interactive on the client; React matches existing DOM instead of re-creating it

title: What are React Server Components (RSC)?
description: What are Server Components?
options: Components that render only on the server with zero client-side JavaScript; can directly access server resources (DB, filesystem); cannot use state or event handlers
correct_answer: Components that render only on the server with zero client-side JavaScript; can directly access server resources (DB, filesystem); cannot use state or event handlers

title: What is useActionState?
description: What does useActionState do in React 19?
options: Manages form action state with pending status; replaces manual useState + useTransition patterns for form submissions
correct_answer: Manages form action state with pending status; replaces manual useState + useTransition patterns for form submissions

title: Implement useThrottle hook.
description: Create a custom hook that throttles a value.

title: Implement useMediaQuery hook.
description: Create a custom hook that listens to a CSS media query.

title: Implement useToggle hook.
description: Create a custom hook that toggles a boolean value.

title: Build a custom useAsync hook.
description: Create a hook that manages async operation state (loading, error, data).

title: What are React portals?
description: What does ReactDOM.createPortal do?
options: Renders children into a different DOM node outside the parent hierarchy; useful for modals, tooltips, and dropdowns that need to break out of overflow or z-index contexts
correct_answer: Renders children into a different DOM node outside the parent hierarchy; useful for modals, tooltips, and dropdowns that need to break out of overflow or z-index contexts

title: What are Error Boundaries?
description: How do Error Boundaries work?
options: Class components that catch JavaScript errors in their child component tree using getDerivedStateFromError and componentDidCatch; they display fallback UI instead of crashing
correct_answer: Class components that catch JavaScript errors in their child component tree using getDerivedStateFromError and componentDidCatch; they display fallback UI instead of crashing

title: What is React.memo?
description: What does React.memo do?
options: A higher-order component that memoizes the component, skipping re-render if props haven't changed (shallow comparison by default)
correct_answer: A higher-order component that memoizes the component, skipping re-render if props haven't changed (shallow comparison by default)

title: What is the difference between React.lazy and dynamic import?
description: How do they relate?
options: React.lazy wraps dynamic import () to create a component that can be rendered with Suspense; dynamic import is the underlying JavaScript feature
correct_answer: React.lazy wraps dynamic import () to create a component that can be rendered with Suspense; dynamic import is the underlying JavaScript feature

title: What is useImperativeHandle?
description: What does useImperativeHandle do?
options: Customizes the instance value exposed to parent components when using ref with forwardRef; limits what the parent can access
correct_answer: Customizes the instance value exposed to parent components when using ref with forwardRef; limits what the parent can access

title: What are synthetic events in React?
description: How do React events differ from native DOM events?
options: React wraps native events in SyntheticEvent objects for cross-browser consistency; they pool events (pre-17) and support the same interface as native events
correct_answer: React wraps native events in SyntheticEvent objects for cross-browser consistency; they pool events (pre-17) and support the same interface as native events

title: What is the React Compiler?
description: What does the new React Compiler (React Forget) do?
options: Automatically memoizes components and hooks at build time, eliminating the need for manual useMemo, useCallback, and React.memo
correct_answer: Automatically memoizes components and hooks at build time, eliminating the need for manual useMemo, useCallback, and React.memo

title: Implement a custom useForm hook.
description: Create a reusable form hook with validation support.

title: Implement virtual scrolling.
description: Create a simple virtual list component that only renders visible items.

title: What is the useDebugValue hook?
description: What does useDebugValue do?
options: Adds a label to custom hooks in React DevTools for easier debugging; only visible in development
correct_answer: Adds a label to custom hooks in React DevTools for easier debugging; only visible in development

title: What is flushSync?
description: What does flushSync do?
options: Forces React to synchronously flush state updates inside the callback, bypassing batching; should be used sparingly as it can hurt performance
correct_answer: Forces React to synchronously flush state updates inside the callback, bypassing batching; should be used sparingly as it can hurt performance

title: What are controlled vs uncontrolled components for file inputs?
description: How do you handle file inputs in React?
options: File inputs are always uncontrolled in React because their value is read-only for security; use onChange + ref to access the FileList
correct_answer: File inputs are always uncontrolled in React because their value is read-only for security; use onChange + ref to access the FileList

title: What is the difference between createElement and JSX?
description: How does JSX relate to React.createElement?
options: JSX is syntactic sugar that compiles to React.createElement (or jsx runtime in React 17+) calls; they produce identical results
correct_answer: JSX is syntactic sugar that compiles to React.createElement (or jsx runtime in React 17+) calls; they produce identical results

title: What is the difference between state and props?
description: How do state and props differ?
options: Props are passed from parent (read-only in child); state is local to the component and can be changed. Both trigger re-renders when they change
correct_answer: Props are passed from parent (read-only in child); state is local to the component and can be changed. Both trigger re-renders when they change

title: What is lifting state up?
description: What does lifting state up mean?
options: Moving shared state to the closest common ancestor component so multiple children can share and sync the same data
correct_answer: Moving shared state to the closest common ancestor component so multiple children can share and sync the same data

title: What is the cleanup function in useEffect?
description: When does the useEffect cleanup run?
options: Runs before the effect re-runs (on dependency change) and on unmount; used to cancel subscriptions, timers, or abort fetch requests
correct_answer: Runs before the effect re-runs (on dependency change) and on unmount; used to cancel subscriptions, timers, or abort fetch requests

title: What is Suspense?
description: What does React Suspense do?
options: Shows a fallback UI while waiting for lazy-loaded components or async data; works with React.lazy, React Server Components, and data fetching libraries
correct_answer: Shows a fallback UI while waiting for lazy-loaded components or async data; works with React.lazy, React Server Components, and data fetching libraries

title: What is the rules of hooks?
description: What are the rules of hooks in React?
options: 1) Only call hooks at the top level (not inside loops/conditions/nested functions). 2) Only call hooks from React function components or custom hooks
correct_answer: 1) Only call hooks at the top level (not inside loops/conditions/nested functions). 2) Only call hooks from React function components or custom hooks

title: What is the startTransition API?
description: What does startTransition do?
options: Marks state updates as non-urgent (transitions), allowing React to keep the UI responsive by interrupting them for urgent updates like typing
correct_answer: Marks state updates as non-urgent (transitions), allowing React to keep the UI responsive by interrupting them for urgent updates like typing
